[[Image:Minamoto no Yoriies grave 2007-02-25.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Minamoto no Yoriie's grave in Shuzenji, [[Izu, Shizuoka|Izu]]]]{{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yoriie'''|源頼家}} ([[September 11]], [[1182]] – [[August 14]], [[1204]]) was the second [[shogun]] (1202–1203) of [[Japan]]'s [[Kamakura shogunate]], and the first son of first shogun [[Minamoto no Yoritomo|Yoritomo]]. His short rule was purely nominal, and he died in Shuzenji, a small town in today's [[Izu province]], assassinated by his uncle [[Hōjō Tokimasa]]. The years in which Yoriie was shogun are encompassed within only one [[Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[nengō]]'': ''[[Kennin]]'' (1201–1204). == Life == [[File:Minamoto no Yoriie.jpg|thumb|left||Minamoto no Yoriie]]Born from Tokimasa's daughter [[Hōjō Masako]] at [[Hiki Yoshikazu]]'s residence in [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura]], Yoriie had as wet nurses the wives of powerful men like Hiki himself and [[Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and Hiki's younger sister.Yasuda (1990:592-593) Before he was born, his father Yoritomo had [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] and his men carry stones to build the [[Tsurugaoka_Hachiman-gū#Wakamiya_.C5.8Cji|Dankazura]] on [[Wakamiya Ōji]] to pray for the child's safe delivery. When Yoriie later himself had a heir, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], the child was also born at the Hiki mansionThe mansion no longer exists and its location ({{coord|35|19|1.31|N|139|33|20.95|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:1500|display=inline}}) is occupied by Myōhon-ji, a temple dedicated to the memory of the Hiki clan, exterminated by the [[Hōjō clan]]. from Hiki's daughter Wakasa no Tsubone, a fact which further consolidating an already strong emotional bondKamiya Vol. 1 (2008:44-45) From this relationship Hiki gained considerable influence when Yoriie became shogun, raising the hostility of [[Hōjō Tokimasa]], who was instead close to Yoriie's younger brother Senman (future third shogun [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]]), and was in his turn trying to leverage that relationship for political advantage.Papinot (2972:385_386) After his father's death in 1199, the 17-year-old became head of the [[Minamoto]] clan and was appointed [[Shogun|''seii taishogun'']] in 1202. He was however criticized for his abandonment of his father's policies, and his mother forbade him to do any political activity. On June 30, 1333 (([[Shōji]] 1, 12th day of the 4th month) his remaining powers were formally taken from him and assumed by a council of 13 elders headed by his grandfather [[Hōjō Tokimasa]]. Yoriie in turn plotted with the Hiki to subjugate the Hōjō clan but failed, was put under house arrest, forced to abdicate and eventually assassinated on July 17, 1204 in Izu. Yoriie was succeeded by his younger brother Sanetomo, the last of the [[Seiwa Genji]] line to rule, at least nominally, over Kamakura. == Hiki Yoshikazu's rebellion== {{main|Hiki Yoshikazu}} Seriously ill, Yoriie proposed to name both his younger brother [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]], and his young son (Hiki's grandson) [[Minamoto no Ichiman]] to succeed him; the two would split power, governing separate parts of the country. It seemed natural to them that Hiki would then be the regent, even if unofficially, of young Ichiman. Hiki suggested to Yoriie, who would be assassinated shortly afterwards by a separate faction (the [[Hōjō clan]], that they arrange to have Sanetomo killed. [[Hōjō Masako]], Yoriie's mother and wife of the first shogun Yoritomo, allegedly overheard the conversation. On a pretext, [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] invited Hiki Yoshikazu to his home and assassinated him. A battle between the clans ensued, the Hiki were defeated by a coalition of the Hōjō, [[Wada clan|Wada]], [[Miura clan|Miura]] and [[Hatakeyama clan]]s and exterminated.Kamakura Citizen's NetAccording to Japanese Wikipedia's "源一幡" (Minamoto no Ichiman) article, the [[Gukanshō]] and the [[Azuma Kagami]] give different versions of the events. This is the Azuma Kagami's version. According to the Gukanshō, when in 1203 Yoriie became seriously ill, the Hōjō clan supported his younger brother Senman (future third shogun Minamoto no Sanetomo) as a successor, while the Hiki supported son Ichiman. To avoid power falling into the hands of the Hiki, the Hōjō decided to get rid of them and their protege. This is the version of events described in the article [[Minamoto no Ichiman]].
According to the same Wikipedia article, the Azuma Kagami claims Ichiman was killed by the Hiki, and not by the Hōjō. == Three sons of Yoriie== Yoriie had three sons, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], [[Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], and Senju-maru, all of whom died of a violent death, victims of the power struggle that followed Yoritomo's sudden death. Ichiman（1198-1203) was the eldest. His mother Wakasa no Tsubone was Hiki Yoshikazu's daughter, and the child was brought up by the Hiki clan and he died in the fire that destroyed the Hiki residence. Second son Yoshinari, the only one of the three to reach adulthood, was forced to become a bonze and in 1219 murdered his uncle Sanetomo on the stone stairs at Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū in the shogunal capital of Kamakura, an act for which he was himself slain on the same day.Kusumoto (2002: 70-73) Third son {{nihongo|Senju-maru|千寿丸|(1201-1214)}} was 12 when Izumi Chikahira rebelled against the Hōjō to make the child shogun. After Chikahira's defeat, the child was forced to become a bonze like his older brother Yoshinari. A year later [[Wada Yoshimori]] also rebelled but, like Chikahira, was defeated and Senju-maru died with the others of the Wada clan. ==Notes== ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * [http://www.kcn-net.org/e_kama_history/omachi/omachi_1.htm Ōmachi], by the Kamakura Citizen's Net, accessed on September 30, 2008 * {{cite book | last = Kusumoto | first = Katsuji | coauthors = | title = Kamakura Naruhodo Jiten | publisher = Jitsugyō no Nihonsha | date = July 2002 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | id = ISBN 978-4-408-00779-3 }} * Papinot, E. (1910). "Historical and Geographical Dictionary of Japan." 1972 Printing. Charles E. Tuttle Company, Tokyo, ISBN 0-8048-0996-8. * {{cite book | last = Yasuda | first = Motohisa (editor) | coauthors = | title = Kamakura, Muromachi Jinmei Jiten | publisher = Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha | date = 1990 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | isbn = 978-4-404-01757-4 | author = | oclc = 24654085 }} * {{cite book | last = Kamiya | first = Michinori | coauthors = | title = Fukaku Aruku - Kamakura Shiseki Sansaku Vol. 1 & 2 | publisher = Kamakura Shunshūsha | date = 2008 | location = Kamakura | language = Japanese | isbn = 4774003409 | oclc = 169992721 }} ===Further reading=== * {{fr icon}} [[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh]], Isaac, ed. (1834). [Siyun-sai Rin-siyo/[[Hayashi Gahō]], 1652], ''[[Nipon o daï itsi ran]]; ou, [http://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&dq=nipon+o+dai+itsi+ran Annales des empereurs du Japon.]'' Paris: [[Royal Asiatic Society|Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland]]. [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/300555357?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 300555357]

revision.comment: [[WP:AWB/T|Typo fixing and general clean up]], typos fixed: a heir → an heir using [[Project:AWB|AWB]]

[[Hōjō Masako]], Yoriie's mother and wife of the first shogun Yoritomo, allegedly overheard the conversation. On a pretext, [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] invited Hiki Yoshikazu to his home and assassinated him. His short rule was purely nominal, and he died in Shuzenji, a small town in today's [[Izu province]], assassinated by his uncle [[Hōjō Tokimasa]]. The years in which Yoriie was shogun are encompassed within only one [[Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[nengō]]'': ''[[Kennin]]'' (1201–1204). == Life == [[File:Minamoto no Yoriie.jpg|thumb|left||Minamoto no Yoriie]]Born from Tokimasa's daughter [[Hōjō Masako]] at [[Hiki Yoshikazu]]'s residence in [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura]], Yoriie had as wet nurses the wives of powerful men like Hiki himself and [[Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and Hiki's younger sister.Yasuda (1990:592-593) Before he was born, his father Yoritomo had [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] and his men carry stones to build the [[Tsurugaoka_Hachiman-gū#Wakamiya_.C5.8Cji|Dankazura]] on [[Wakamiya Ōji]] to pray for the child's safe delivery. When Yoriie later himself had an heir, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], the child was also born at the Hiki mansionThe mansion no longer exists and its location ({{coord|35|19|1.31|N|139|33|20.95|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:1500|display=inline}}) is occupied by Myōhon-ji, a temple dedicated to the memory of the Hiki clan, exterminated by the [[Hōjō clan]]. from Hiki's daughter Wakasa no Tsubone, a fact which further consolidating an already strong emotional bondKamiya Vol. 1 (2008:44-45) From this relationship Hiki gained considerable influence when Yoriie became shogun, raising the hostility of [[Hōjō Tokimasa]], who was instead close to Yoriie's younger brother Senman (future third shogun [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]]), and was in his turn trying to leverage that relationship for political advantage.Papinot (2972:385_386) After his father's death in 1199, the 17-year-old became head of the [[Minamoto]] clan and was appointed [[Shogun|''seii taishogun'']] in 1202. He was however criticized for his abandonment of his father's policies, and his mother forbade him to do any political activity. On June 30, 1333 ([[Shōji]] 1, 12th day of the 4th month) his remaining powers were formally taken from him and assumed by a council of 13 elders headed by his grandfather [[Hōjō Tokimasa]]. Yoriie in turn plotted with the Hiki to subjugate the Hōjō clan but failed, was put under house arrest, forced to abdicate and eventually assassinated on July 17, 1204 in Izu. Yoriie was succeeded by his younger brother Sanetomo, the last of the [[Seiwa Genji]] line to rule, at least nominally, over Kamakura. == Hiki Yoshikazu's rebellion== {{main|Hiki Yoshikazu}} Seriously ill, Yoriie proposed to name both his younger brother [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]], and his young son (Hiki's grandson) [[Minamoto no Ichiman]] to succeed him; the two would split power, governing separate parts of the country. It seemed natural to them that Hiki would then be the regent, even if unofficially, of young Ichiman. Hiki suggested to Yoriie, who would be assassinated shortly afterwards by a separate faction (the [[Hōjō clan]], that they arrange to have Sanetomo killed. [[Hōjō Masako]], Yoriie's mother and wife of the first shogun Yoritomo, allegedly overheard the conversation. On a pretext, [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] invited Hiki Yoshikazu to his home and assassinated him. A battle between the clans ensued, the Hiki were defeated by a coalition of the Hōjō, [[Wada clan|Wada]], [[Miura clan|Miura]] and [[Hatakeyama clan]]s and exterminated.Kamakura Citizen's NetAccording to Japanese Wikipedia's "源一幡" (Minamoto no Ichiman) article, the [[Gukanshō]] and the [[Azuma Kagami]] give different versions of the events. This is the Azuma Kagami's version. According to the Gukanshō, when in 1203 Yoriie became seriously ill, the Hōjō clan supported his younger brother Senman (future third shogun Minamoto no Sanetomo) as a successor, while the Hiki supported son Ichiman. To avoid power falling into the hands of the Hiki, the Hōjō decided to get rid of them and their protege. This is the version of events described in the article [[Minamoto no Ichiman]].
According to the same Wikipedia article, the Azuma Kagami claims Ichiman was killed by the Hiki, and not by the Hōjō. == Three sons of Yoriie== Yoriie had three sons, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], [[Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], and Senju-maru, all of whom died of a violent death, victims of the power struggle that followed Yoritomo's sudden death. Ichiman（1198-1203) was the eldest. His mother Wakasa no Tsubone was Hiki Yoshikazu's daughter, and the child was brought up by the Hiki clan and he died in the fire that destroyed the Hiki residence. Second son Yoshinari, the only one of the three to reach adulthood, was forced to become a bonze and in 1219 murdered his uncle Sanetomo on the stone stairs at Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū in the shogunal capital of Kamakura, an act for which he was himself slain on the same day.Kusumoto (2002: 70-73) Third son {{nihongo|Senju-maru|千寿丸|(1201-1214)}} was 12 when Izumi Chikahira rebelled against the Hōjō to make the child shogun. After Chikahira's defeat, the child was forced to become a bonze like his older brother Yoshinari. A year later [[Wada Yoshimori]] also rebelled but, like Chikahira, was defeated and Senju-maru died with the others of the Wada clan. ==Notes== ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * [http://www.kcn-net.org/e_kama_history/omachi/omachi_1.htm Ōmachi], by the Kamakura Citizen's Net, accessed on September 30, 2008 * {{cite book | last = Kusumoto | first = Katsuji | coauthors = | title = Kamakura Naruhodo Jiten | publisher = Jitsugyō no Nihonsha | date = July 2002 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | id = ISBN 978-4-408-00779-3 }} * Papinot, E. (1910). "Historical and Geographical Dictionary of Japan." 1972 Printing. Charles E. Tuttle Company, Tokyo, ISBN 0-8048-0996-8. * {{cite book | last = Yasuda | first = Motohisa (editor) | coauthors = | title = Kamakura, Muromachi Jinmei Jiten | publisher = Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha | year = 1990 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | isbn = 978-4-404-01757-4 | author = | oclc = 24654085 }} * {{cite book | last = Kamiya | first = Michinori | coauthors = | title = Fukaku Aruku - Kamakura Shiseki Sansaku Vol. 1 & 2 | publisher = Kamakura Shunshūsha | year = 2008 | location = Kamakura | language = Japanese | isbn = 4774003409 | oclc = 169992721 }} ===Further reading=== * {{fr icon}} [[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh]], Isaac, ed. (1834). [Siyun-sai Rin-siyo/[[Hayashi Gahō]], 1652], ''[[Nipon o daï itsi ran]]; ou, [http://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&dq=nipon+o+dai+itsi+ran Annales des empereurs du Japon.]'' Paris: [[Royal Asiatic Society|Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland]]. [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/300555357?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 300555357]

The years in which Yoriie was shogun are encompassed within only one [[Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[nengō]]'': ''[[Kennin]]'' (1201–1204). == Life == [[File:Minamoto no Yoriie.jpg|thumb|left||Minamoto no Yoriie]]Born from Tokimasa's daughter [[Hōjō Masako]] at [[Hiki Yoshikazu]]'s residence in [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura]], Yoriie had as wet nurses the wives of powerful men like Hiki himself and [[Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and Hiki's younger sister.Yasuda (1990:592-593) Before he was born, his father Yoritomo had [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] and his men carry stones to build the [[Tsurugaoka_Hachiman-gū#Wakamiya_.C5.8Cji|Dankazura]] on [[Wakamiya Ōji]] to pray for the child's safe delivery. When Yoriie later himself had an heir, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], the child was also born at the Hiki mansionThe mansion no longer exists and its location ({{coord|35|19|1.31|N|139|33|20.95|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:1500|display=inline}}) is occupied by Myōhon-ji, a temple dedicated to the memory of the Hiki clan, exterminated by the [[Hōjō clan]]. from Hiki's daughter Wakasa no Tsubone, a fact which further consolidating an already strong emotional bondKamiya Vol. 1 (2008:44-45) From this relationship Hiki gained considerable influence when Yoriie became shogun, raising the hostility of [[Hōjō Tokimasa]], who was instead close to Yoriie's younger brother Senman (future third shogun [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]]), and was in his turn trying to leverage that relationship for political advantage.Papinot (2972:385_386) After his father's death in 1199, the 17-year-old became head of the [[Minamoto]] clan and was appointed [[Shogun|''seii taishogun'']] in 1202. He was however criticized for his abandonment of his father's policies, and his mother forbade him to do any political activity. On June 30, 1333 ([[Shōji]] 1, 12th day of the 4th month) his remaining powers were formally taken from him and assumed by a council of 13 elders headed by his grandfather [[Hōjō Tokimasa]]. Yoriie in turn plotted with the Hiki to subjugate the Hōjō clan but failed, was put under house arrest, forced to abdicate and eventually assassinated on July 17, 1204 in Izu. Yoriie was succeeded by his younger brother Sanetomo, the last of the [[Seiwa Genji]] line to rule, at least nominally, over Kamakura. == Hiki Yoshikazu's rebellion== {{main|Hiki Yoshikazu}} Seriously ill, Yoriie proposed to name both his younger brother [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]], and his young son (Hiki's grandson) [[Minamoto no Ichiman]] to succeed him; the two would split power, governing separate parts of the country. It seemed natural to them that Hiki would then be the regent, even if unofficially, of young Ichiman. Hiki suggested to Yoriie, who would be assassinated shortly afterwards by a separate faction (the [[Hōjō clan]], that they arrange to have Sanetomo killed. [[Hōjō Masako]], Yoriie's mother and wife of the first shogun Yoritomo, allegedly overheard the conversation. On a pretext, [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] invited Hiki Yoshikazu to his home and assassinated him. A battle between the clans ensued, the Hiki were defeated by a coalition of the Hōjō, [[Wada clan|Wada]], [[Miura clan|Miura]] and [[Hatakeyama clan]]s and exterminated.Kamakura Citizen's NetAccording to Japanese Wikipedia's "源一幡" (Minamoto no Ichiman) article, the [[Gukanshō]] and the [[Azuma Kagami]] give different versions of the events. This is the Azuma Kagami's version. According to the Gukanshō, when in 1203 Yoriie became seriously ill, the Hōjō clan supported his younger brother Senman (future third shogun Minamoto no Sanetomo) as a successor, while the Hiki supported son Ichiman. To avoid power falling into the hands of the Hiki, the Hōjō decided to get rid of them and their protege. This is the version of events described in the article [[Minamoto no Ichiman]].
According to the same Wikipedia article, the Azuma Kagami claims Ichiman was killed by the Hiki, and not by the Hōjō. == Three sons of Yoriie== Yoriie had three sons, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], [[Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], and Senju-maru, all of whom died of a violent death, victims of the power struggle that followed Yoritomo's sudden death. Ichiman（1198-1203) was the eldest. His mother Wakasa no Tsubone was Hiki Yoshikazu's daughter, and the child was brought up by the Hiki clan and he died in the fire that destroyed the Hiki residence. Second son Yoshinari, the only one of the three to reach adulthood, was forced to become a bonze and in 1219 murdered his uncle Sanetomo on the stone stairs at Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū in the shogunal capital of Kamakura, an act for which he was himself slain on the same day.Kusumoto (2002: 70-73) Third son {{nihongo|Senju-maru|千寿丸|(1201-1214)}} was 12 when Izumi Chikahira rebelled against the Hōjō to make the child shogun. After Chikahira's defeat, the child was forced to become a bonze like his older brother Yoshinari. A year later [[Wada Yoshimori]] also rebelled but, like Chikahira, was defeated and Senju-maru died with the others of the Wada clan. ==Notes== ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * [http://www.kcn-net.org/e_kama_history/omachi/omachi_1.htm Ōmachi], by the Kamakura Citizen's Net, accessed on September 30, 2008 * {{cite book | last = Kusumoto | first = Katsuji | coauthors = | title = Kamakura Naruhodo Jiten | publisher = Jitsugyō no Nihonsha | date = July 2002 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | id = ISBN 978-4-408-00779-3 }} * Papinot, E. (1910). "Historical and Geographical Dictionary of Japan." 1972 Printing. Charles E. Tuttle Company, Tokyo, ISBN 0-8048-0996-8. * {{cite book | last = Yasuda | first = Motohisa (editor) | coauthors = | title = Kamakura, Muromachi Jinmei Jiten | publisher = Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha | year = 1990 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | isbn = 978-4-404-01757-4 | author = | oclc = 24654085 }} * {{cite book | last = Kamiya | first = Michinori | coauthors = | title = Fukaku Aruku - Kamakura Shiseki Sansaku Vol. 1 & 2 | publisher = Kamakura Shunshūsha | year = 2008 | location = Kamakura | language = Japanese | isbn = 4774003409 | oclc = 169992721 }} ===Further reading=== * {{fr icon}} [[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh]], Isaac, ed. (1834). [Siyun-sai Rin-siyo/[[Hayashi Gahō]], 1652], ''[[Nipon o daï itsi ran]]; ou, [http://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&dq=nipon+o+dai+itsi+ran Annales des empereurs du Japon.]'' Paris: [[Royal Asiatic Society|Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland]]. [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/300555357?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 300555357]

revision.comment: Undid revision 387604485 by [[Special:Contributions/SpikeToronto|SpikeToronto]] ([[User talk:SpikeToronto|talk]]) The 1333 date is a typo. Yoriie died in 1204.
revision.text:
[[Image:Minamoto no Yoriies grave 2007-02-25.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Minamoto no Yoriie's grave in Shuzenji, [[Izu, Shizuoka|Izu]]]] {{Japanese name|Minamoto}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yoriie'''|源 頼家}} ([[September 11]], [[1182]] – [[August 14]], [[1204]]) was the second [[shogun]] (1202–1203) of [[Japan]]'s [[Kamakura shogunate]], and the first son of first shogun [[Minamoto no Yoritomo|Yoritomo]]. His short rule was purely nominal, and he died in Shuzenji, a small town in today's [[Izu province]], assassinated by his uncle [[Hōjō Tokimasa]]. The years in which Yoriie was shogun are encompassed within only one [[Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[nengō]]'': ''[[Kennin]]'' (1201–1204). == Life == [[File:Minamoto no Yoriie.jpg|thumb|left||Minamoto no Yoriie]]Born from Tokimasa's daughter [[Hōjō Masako]] at [[Hiki Yoshikazu]]'s residence in [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura]], Yoriie had as wet nurses the wives of powerful men like Hiki himself and [[Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and Hiki's younger sister.Yasuda (1990:592-593) Before he was born, his father Yoritomo had [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] and his men carry stones to build the [[Tsurugaoka_Hachiman-gū#Wakamiya_.C5.8Cji|Dankazura]] on [[Wakamiya Ōji]] to pray for the child's safe delivery. When Yoriie later himself had an heir, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], the child was also born at the Hiki mansionThe mansion no longer exists and its location ({{coord|35|19|1.31|N|139|33|20.95|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:1500|display=inline}}) is occupied by Myōhon-ji, a temple dedicated to the memory of the Hiki clan, exterminated by the [[Hōjō clan]]. from Hiki's daughter Wakasa no Tsubone, a fact which further consolidating an already strong emotional bondKamiya Vol. 1 (2008:44-45) From this relationship Hiki gained considerable influence when Yoriie became shogun, raising the hostility of [[Hōjō Tokimasa]], who was instead close to Yoriie's younger brother Senman (future third shogun [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]]), and was in his turn trying to leverage that relationship for political advantage.Papinot (2972:385_386) After his father's death in 1199, the 17-year-old became head of the [[Minamoto]] clan and was appointed [[Shogun|''seii taishogun'']] in 1202. He was however criticized for his abandonment of his father's policies, and his mother forbade him to do any political activity. On June 30, 1203 ([[Shōji]] 1, 12th day of the 4th month) his remaining powers were formally taken from him and assumed by a council of 13 elders headed by his grandfather [[Hōjō Tokimasa]]. Yoriie in turn plotted with the Hiki to subjugate the Hōjō clan but failed, was put under house arrest, forced to abdicate and eventually assassinated on July 17, 1204 in Izu. Yoriie was succeeded by his younger brother Sanetomo, the last of the [[Seiwa Genji]] line to rule, at least nominally, over Kamakura. == Hiki Yoshikazu's rebellion== {{main|Hiki Yoshikazu}} Seriously ill, Yoriie proposed to name both his younger brother [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]], and his young son (Hiki's grandson) [[Minamoto no Ichiman]] to succeed him; the two would split power, governing separate parts of the country. It seemed natural to them that Hiki would then be the regent, even if unofficially, of young Ichiman. Hiki suggested to Yoriie, who would be assassinated shortly afterwards by a separate faction (the [[Hōjō clan]], that they arrange to have Sanetomo killed. [[Hōjō Masako]], Yoriie's mother and wife of the first shogun Yoritomo, allegedly overheard the conversation. On a pretext, [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] invited Hiki Yoshikazu to his home and assassinated him. A battle between the clans ensued, the Hiki were defeated by a coalition of the Hōjō, [[Wada clan|Wada]], [[Miura clan|Miura]] and [[Hatakeyama clan]]s and exterminated.Kamakura Citizen's NetAccording to Japanese Wikipedia's "源一幡" (Minamoto no Ichiman) article, the [[Gukanshō]] and the [[Azuma Kagami]] give different versions of the events. This is the Azuma Kagami's version. According to the Gukanshō, when in 1203 Yoriie became seriously ill, the Hōjō clan supported his younger brother Senman (future third shogun Minamoto no Sanetomo) as a successor, while the Hiki supported son Ichiman. To avoid power falling into the hands of the Hiki, the Hōjō decided to get rid of them and their protege. This is the version of events described in the article [[Minamoto no Ichiman]].
According to the same Wikipedia article, the Azuma Kagami claims Ichiman was killed by the Hiki, and not by the Hōjō. == Three sons of Yoriie== Yoriie had three sons, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], [[Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], and Senju-maru, all of whom died of a violent death, victims of the power struggle that followed Yoritomo's sudden death. Ichiman（1198-1203) was the eldest. His mother Wakasa no Tsubone was Hiki Yoshikazu's daughter, and the child was brought up by the Hiki clan and he died in the fire that destroyed the Hiki residence. Second son Yoshinari, the only one of the three to reach adulthood, was forced to become a bonze and in 1219 murdered his uncle Sanetomo on the stone stairs at Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū in the shogunal capital of Kamakura, an act for which he was himself slain on the same day.Kusumoto (2002: 70-73) Third son {{nihongo|Senju-maru|千寿丸|(1201-1214)}} was 12 when Izumi Chikahira rebelled against the Hōjō to make the child shogun. After Chikahira's defeat, the child was forced to become a bonze like his older brother Yoshinari. A year later [[Wada Yoshimori]] also rebelled but, like Chikahira, was defeated and Senju-maru died with the others of the Wada clan. ==Notes== ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * [http://www.kcn-net.org/e_kama_history/omachi/omachi_1.htm Ōmachi], by the Kamakura Citizen's Net, accessed on September 30, 2008 * {{cite book | last = Kusumoto | first = Katsuji | coauthors = | title = Kamakura Naruhodo Jiten | publisher = Jitsugyō no Nihonsha | date = July 2002 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | id = ISBN 978-4-408-00779-3 }} * Papinot, E. When Yoriie later himself had an heir, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], the child was also born at the Hiki mansionThe mansion no longer exists and its location ({{coord|35|19|1.31|N|139|33|20.95|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:1500|display=inline}}) is occupied by Myōhon-ji, a temple dedicated to the memory of the Hiki clan, exterminated by the [[Hōjō clan]]. from Hiki's daughter Wakasa no Tsubone, a fact which further consolidating an already strong emotional bondKamiya Vol. 1 (2008:44-45) From this relationship Hiki gained considerable influence when Yoriie became shogun, raising the hostility of [[Hōjō Tokimasa]], who was instead close to Yoriie's younger brother Senman (future third shogun [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]]), and was in his turn trying to leverage that relationship for political advantage.Papinot (2972:385_386) After his father's death in 1199, the 17-year-old became head of the [[Minamoto]] clan and was appointed [[Shogun|''seii taishogun'']] in 1202. He was however criticized for his abandonment of his father's policies, and his mother forbade him to do any political activity. On June 30, 1203 ([[Shōji]] 1, 12th day of the 4th month) his remaining powers were formally taken from him and assumed by a council of 13 elders headed by his grandfather [[Hōjō Tokimasa]]. Yoriie in turn plotted with the Hiki to subjugate the Hōjō clan but failed, was put under house arrest, forced to abdicate and eventually assassinated on July 17, 1204 in Izu. Yoriie was succeeded by his younger brother Sanetomo, the last of the [[Seiwa Genji]] line to rule, at least nominally, over Kamakura. == Hiki Yoshikazu's rebellion== {{main|Hiki Yoshikazu}} Seriously ill, Yoriie proposed to name both his younger brother [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]], and his young son (Hiki's grandson) [[Minamoto no Ichiman]] to succeed him; the two would split power, governing separate parts of the country. It seemed natural to them that Hiki would then be the regent, even if unofficially, of young Ichiman. Hiki suggested to Yoriie, who would be assassinated shortly afterwards by a separate faction (the [[Hōjō clan]], that they arrange to have Sanetomo killed. [[Hōjō Masako]], Yoriie's mother and wife of the first shogun Yoritomo, allegedly overheard the conversation. On a pretext, [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] invited Hiki Yoshikazu to his home and assassinated him. A battle between the clans ensued, the Hiki were defeated by a coalition of the Hōjō, [[Wada clan|Wada]], [[Miura clan|Miura]] and [[Hatakeyama clan]]s and exterminated.Kamakura Citizen's NetAccording to Japanese Wikipedia's "源一幡" (Minamoto no Ichiman) article, the [[Gukanshō]] and the [[Azuma Kagami]] give different versions of the events. This is the Azuma Kagami's version. According to the Gukanshō, when in 1203 Yoriie became seriously ill, the Hōjō clan supported his younger brother Senman (future third shogun Minamoto no Sanetomo) as a successor, while the Hiki supported son Ichiman. To avoid power falling into the hands of the Hiki, the Hōjō decided to get rid of them and their protege. This is the version of events described in the article [[Minamoto no Ichiman]].
According to the same Wikipedia article, the Azuma Kagami claims Ichiman was killed by the Hiki, and not by the Hōjō. == Three sons of Yoriie== Yoriie had three sons, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], [[Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], and Senju-maru, all of whom died of a violent death, victims of the power struggle that followed Yoritomo's sudden death. Ichiman(1198-1203) was the eldest. His mother Wakasa no Tsubone was Hiki Yoshikazu's daughter, and the child was brought up by the Hiki clan and he died in the fire that destroyed the Hiki residence. Second son Yoshinari, the only one of the three to reach adulthood, was forced to become a bonze and in 1219 murdered his uncle Sanetomo on the stone stairs at Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū in the shogunal capital of Kamakura, an act for which he was himself slain on the same day.Kusumoto (2002: 70-73) Third son {{nihongo|Senju-maru|千寿丸|(1201-1214)}} was 12 when Izumi Chikahira rebelled against the Hōjō to make the child shogun. After Chikahira's defeat, the child was forced to become a bonze like his older brother Yoshinari. A year later [[Wada Yoshimori]] also rebelled but, like Chikahira, was defeated and Senju-maru died with the others of the Wada clan. ==Notes== ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * [http://www.kcn-net.org/e_kama_history/omachi/omachi_1.htm Ōmachi], by the Kamakura Citizen's Net, accessed on September 30, 2008 * {{cite book | last = Kusumoto | first = Katsuji | coauthors = | title = Kamakura Naruhodo Jiten | publisher = Jitsugyō no Nihonsha | date = July 2002 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | id = ISBN 978-4-408-00779-3 }} * Papinot, E. (1910). "Historical and Geographical Dictionary of Japan." 1972 Printing. Charles E. Tuttle Company, Tokyo, ISBN 0-8048-0996-8. * {{cite book | last = Yasuda | first = Motohisa (editor) | coauthors = | title = Kamakura, Muromachi Jinmei Jiten | publisher = Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha | year = 1990 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | isbn = 978-4-404-01757-4 | author = | oclc = 24654085 }} * {{cite book | last = Kamiya | first = Michinori | coauthors = | title = Fukaku Aruku - Kamakura Shiseki Sansaku Vol. 1 & 2 | publisher = Kamakura Shunshūsha | year = 2008 | location = Kamakura | language = Japanese | isbn = 4774003409 | oclc = 169992721 }} ===Further reading=== * {{fr icon}} [[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh]], Isaac, ed. (1834). [Siyun-sai Rin-siyo/[[Hayashi Gahō]], 1652], ''[[Nipon o daï itsi ran]]; ou, [http://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&dq=nipon+o+dai+itsi+ran Annales des empereurs du Japon.]'' Paris: [[Royal Asiatic Society|Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland]]. [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/300555357?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 300555357]

[[Image:Minamoto no Yoriies grave 2007-02-25.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Minamoto no Yoriie's grave in Shuzenji, [[Izu, Shizuoka|Izu]]]] {{Japanese name|Minamoto}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yoriie'''|源 頼家}} ([[September 11]], [[1182]] – [[August 14]], [[1204]]) was the second [[shogun]] (1202–1203) of [[Japan]]'s [[Kamakura shogunate]], and the first son of first shogun [[Minamoto no Yoritomo|Yoritomo]]. His short rule was purely nominal and he died in Shuzenji, a small town in today's [[Izu province]], assassinated by his uncle [[Hōjō Tokimasa]]. The years in which Yoriie was shogun are all within only one [[Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[nengō]]'': ''[[Kennin]]'' (1201–1204). == Life == [[File:Minamoto no Yoriie.jpg|thumb|left||Minamoto no Yoriie]]Born from Tokimasa's daughter [[Hōjō Masako]] at [[Hiki Yoshikazu]]'s residence in [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura]], Yoriie had as wet nurses the wives of powerful men like Hiki himself and [[Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and Hiki's younger sister.Yasuda (1990:592-593) Before he was born, his father Yoritomo had [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] and his men carry stones to build the [[Tsurugaoka_Hachiman-gū#Wakamiya_.C5.8Cji|Dankazura]] on [[Wakamiya Ōji]] to pray for the child's safe delivery. When Yoriie later himself had an heir, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], the child was also born at the Hiki mansionThe mansion no longer exists and its location ({{coord|35|19|1.31|N|139|33|20.95|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:1500|display=inline}}) is occupied by Myōhon-ji, a temple dedicated to the memory of the Hiki clan, exterminated by the [[Hōjō clan]]. from Hiki's daughter Wakasa no Tsubone, a fact which further consolidating an already strong emotional bondKamiya Vol. 1 (2008:44-45) From this relationship Hiki gained considerable influence when Yoriie became shogun, raising the hostility of [[Hōjō Tokimasa]], who was instead close to Yoriie's younger brother Senman (future third shogun [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]]), and was in his turn trying to leverage that relationship for political advantage.Papinot (2972:385_386) After his father's death in 1199, the 17-year-old became head of the [[Minamoto]] clan and was appointed [[Shogun|''seii taishogun'']] in 1202. It seemed natural to them that Hiki would then be the regent, even if unofficially, of young Ichiman. Hiki suggested to Yoriie, who would be assassinated shortly afterwards by a separate faction (the [[Hōjō clan]], that they arrange to have Sanetomo killed. [[Hōjō Masako]], Yoriie's mother and wife of the first shogun Yoritomo, allegedly overheard the conversation. On a pretext, [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] invited Hiki Yoshikazu to his home and assassinated him. A battle between the clans ensued, the Hiki were defeated by a coalition of the Hōjō, [[Wada clan|Wada]], [[Miura clan|Miura]] and [[Hatakeyama clan]]s and exterminated.Kamakura Citizen's NetAccording to Japanese Wikipedia's "源一幡" (Minamoto no Ichiman) article, the [[Gukanshō]] and the [[Azuma Kagami]] give different versions of the events. To avoid power falling into the hands of the Hiki, the Hōjō decided to get rid of them and their protege. This is the version of events described in the article [[Minamoto no Ichiman]].
According to the same Wikipedia article, the Azuma Kagami claims Ichiman was killed by the Hiki, and not by the Hōjō. == Three sons of Yoriie== Yoriie had three sons, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], [[Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], and Senju-maru, all of whom died of a violent death, victims of the power struggle that followed Yoritomo's sudden death. He was however criticized for his abandonment of his father's policies, and his mother forbade him to do any political activity. On June 30, 1203 ([[Shōji]] 1, 12th day of the 4th month) his remaining powers were formally taken from him and assumed by a council of 13 elders headed by his grandfather [[Hōjō Tokimasa]]. Yoriie in turn plotted with the Hiki to subjugate the Hōjō clan but failed, was put under house arrest, forced to abdicate and eventually assassinated on July 17, 1204 in Izu. Yoriie was succeeded by his younger brother Sanetomo, the last of the [[Seiwa Genji]] line to rule, at least nominally, over Kamakura. == Hiki Yoshikazu's rebellion== {{main|Hiki Yoshikazu}} Seriously ill, Yoriie proposed to name both his younger brother [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]], and his young son (Hiki's grandson) [[Minamoto no Ichiman]] to succeed him; the two would split power, governing separate parts of the country. It seemed natural to them that Hiki would then be the regent, even if unofficially, of young Ichiman. Hiki suggested to Yoriie, who would be assassinated shortly afterwards by a separate faction (the [[Hōjō clan]], that they arrange to have Sanetomo killed. [[Hōjō Masako]], Yoriie's mother and wife of the first shogun Yoritomo, allegedly overheard the conversation. On a pretext, [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] invited Hiki Yoshikazu to his home and assassinated him. A battle between the clans ensued, the Hiki were defeated by a coalition of the Hōjō, [[Wada clan|Wada]], [[Miura clan|Miura]] and [[Hatakeyama clan]]s and exterminated.Kamakura Citizen's NetAccording to Japanese Wikipedia's "源一幡" (Minamoto no Ichiman) article, the [[Gukanshō]] and the [[Azuma Kagami]] give different versions of the events. This is the Azuma Kagami's version. According to the Gukanshō, when in 1203 Yoriie became seriously ill, the Hōjō clan supported his younger brother Senman (future third shogun Minamoto no Sanetomo) as a successor, while the Hiki supported son Ichiman. To avoid power falling into the hands of the Hiki, the Hōjō decided to get rid of them and their protege. This is the version of events described in the article [[Minamoto no Ichiman]].
According to the same Wikipedia article, the Azuma Kagami claims Ichiman was killed by the Hiki, and not by the Hōjō. == Three sons of Yoriie== Yoriie had three sons, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], [[Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], and Senju-maru, all of whom died of a violent death, victims of the power struggle that followed Yoritomo's sudden death. Ichiman (1198-1203) was the eldest. His mother Wakasa no Tsubone was Hiki Yoshikazu's daughter, and the child was brought up by the Hiki clan. There are contrasting versions of his deatth, but in any case he died in the fire that destroyed the Hiki residence. Second son Yoshinari, the only one of the three to reach adulthood, was forced to become a bonze and in 1219 murdered his uncle Sanetomo on the stone stairs at Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū in the shogunal capital of Kamakura, an act for which he was himself slain on the same day.Kusumoto (2002: 70-73) Third son {{nihongo|Senju-maru|千寿丸|(1201-1214)}} was 12 when Izumi Chikahira rebelled against the Hōjō to make the child shogun. After Chikahira's defeat, the child was forced to become a bonze like his older brother Yoshinari. A year later [[Wada Yoshimori]] also rebelled but, like Chikahira, was defeated and Senju-maru died with the others of the Wada clan. ==Notes== ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * [http://www.kcn-net.org/e_kama_history/omachi/omachi_1.htm Ōmachi], by the Kamakura Citizen's Net, accessed on September 30, 2008 * {{cite book | last = Kusumoto | first = Katsuji | coauthors = | title = Kamakura Naruhodo Jiten | publisher = Jitsugyō no Nihonsha | date = July 2002 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | id = ISBN 978-4-408-00779-3 }} * Papinot, E. (1910). "Historical and Geographical Dictionary of Japan." 1972 Printing. Charles E. Tuttle Company, Tokyo, ISBN 0-8048-0996-8. * {{cite book | last = Yasuda | first = Motohisa (editor) | coauthors = | title = Kamakura, Muromachi Jinmei Jiten | publisher = Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha | year = 1990 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | isbn = 978-4-404-01757-4 | author = | oclc = 24654085 }} * {{cite book | last = Kamiya | first = Michinori | coauthors = | title = Fukaku Aruku - Kamakura Shiseki Sansaku Vol. 1 & 2 | publisher = Kamakura Shunshūsha | year = 2008 | location = Kamakura | language = Japanese | isbn = 4774003409 | oclc = 169992721 }} ===Further reading=== * {{fr icon}} [[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh]], Isaac, ed. [[Image:Minamoto no Yoriies grave 2007-02-25.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Minamoto no Yoriie's grave in Shuzenji, [[Izu, Shizuoka|Izu]]]] {{Japanese name|Minamoto}} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yoriie'''|源 頼家||September 11, 1182 – August 14, 1204}} was the second [[shogun]] (1202–1203) of [[Japan]]'s [[Kamakura shogunate]], and the first son of first shogun [[Minamoto no Yoritomo|Yoritomo]]. His short rule was purely nominal and he died in Shuzenji, a small town in today's [[Izu province]], assassinated by his uncle [[Hōjō Tokimasa]]. The years in which Yoriie was shogun are all within only one [[Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[nengō]]'': ''[[Kennin]]'' (1201–1204). == Life == [[File:Minamoto no Yoriie.jpg|thumb|left||Minamoto no Yoriie]]Born from Tokimasa's daughter [[Hōjō Masako]] at [[Hiki Yoshikazu]]'s residence in [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura]], Yoriie had as wet nurses the wives of powerful men like Hiki himself and [[Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and Hiki's younger sister.Yasuda (1990:592-593) Before he was born, his father Yoritomo had [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] and his men carry stones to build the [[Tsurugaoka_Hachiman-gū#Wakamiya_.C5.8Cji|Dankazura]] on [[Wakamiya Ōji]] to pray for the child's safe delivery. When Yoriie later himself had an heir, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], the child was also born at the Hiki mansionThe mansion no longer exists and its location ({{coord|35|19|1.31|N|139|33|20.95|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:1500|display=inline}}) is occupied by Myōhon-ji, a temple dedicated to the memory of the Hiki clan, exterminated by the [[Hōjō clan]]. from Hiki's daughter Wakasa no Tsubone, a fact which further consolidating an already strong emotional bondKamiya Vol. 1 (2008:44-45) From this relationship Hiki gained considerable influence when Yoriie became shogun, raising the hostility of [[Hōjō Tokimasa]], who was instead close to Yoriie's younger brother Senman (future third shogun [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]]), and was in his turn trying to leverage that relationship for political advantage.Papinot (2972:385_386) After his father's death in 1199, the 17-year-old became head of the [[Minamoto]] clan and was appointed [[Shogun|''seii taishogun'']] in 1202. He was however criticized for his abandonment of his father's policies, and his mother forbade him to do any political activity. On June 30, 1203 ([[Shōji]] 1, 12th day of the 4th month) his remaining powers were formally taken from him and assumed by a council of 13 elders headed by his grandfather [[Hōjō Tokimasa]]. Yoriie in turn plotted with the Hiki to subjugate the Hōjō clan but failed, was put under house arrest, forced to abdicate and eventually assassinated on July 17, 1204 in Izu. Yoriie was succeeded by his younger brother Sanetomo, the last of the [[Seiwa Genji]] line to rule, at least nominally, over Kamakura. == Hiki Yoshikazu's rebellion== {{main|Hiki Yoshikazu}} Seriously ill, Yoriie proposed to name both his younger brother [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]], and his young son (Hiki's grandson) [[Minamoto no Ichiman]] to succeed him; the two would split power, governing separate parts of the country. It seemed natural to them that Hiki would then be the regent, even if unofficially, of young Ichiman. Hiki suggested to Yoriie, who would be assassinated shortly afterwards by a separate faction (the [[Hōjō clan]], that they arrange to have Sanetomo killed. [[Hōjō Masako]], Yoriie's mother and wife of the first shogun Yoritomo, allegedly overheard the conversation. On a pretext, [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] invited Hiki Yoshikazu to his home and assassinated him. A battle between the clans ensued, the Hiki were defeated by a coalition of the Hōjō, [[Wada clan|Wada]], [[Miura clan|Miura]] and [[Hatakeyama clan]]s and exterminated.Kamakura Citizen's NetAccording to Japanese Wikipedia's "源一幡" (Minamoto no Ichiman) article, the [[Gukanshō]] and the [[Azuma Kagami]] give different versions of the events. This is the Azuma Kagami's version. According to the Gukanshō, when in 1203 Yoriie became seriously ill, the Hōjō clan supported his younger brother Senman (future third shogun Minamoto no Sanetomo) as a successor, while the Hiki supported son Ichiman. To avoid power falling into the hands of the Hiki, the Hōjō decided to get rid of them and their protege. This is the version of events described in the article [[Minamoto no Ichiman]].
According to the same Wikipedia article, the Azuma Kagami claims Ichiman was killed by the Hiki, and not by the Hōjō. == Three sons of Yoriie== Yoriie had three sons, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], [[Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], and Senju-maru, all of whom died of a violent death, victims of the power struggle that followed Yoritomo's sudden death. Ichiman (1198-1203) was the eldest. His mother Wakasa no Tsubone was Hiki Yoshikazu's daughter, and the child was brought up by the Hiki clan. There are contrasting versions of his deatth, but in any case he died in the fire that destroyed the Hiki residence. Second son Yoshinari, the only one of the three to reach adulthood, was forced to become a bonze and in 1219 murdered his uncle Sanetomo on the stone stairs at Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū in the shogunal capital of Kamakura, an act for which he was himself slain on the same day.Kusumoto (2002: 70-73) Third son {{nihongo|Senju-maru|千寿丸|(1201-1214)}} was 12 when Izumi Chikahira rebelled against the Hōjō to make the child shogun. After Chikahira's defeat, the child was forced to become a bonze like his older brother Yoshinari. A year later [[Wada Yoshimori]] also rebelled but, like Chikahira, was defeated and Senju-maru died with the others of the Wada clan. ==Notes== ==References== {{Reflist}} ==Bibliography== * [http://www.kcn-net.org/e_kama_history/omachi/omachi_1.htm Ōmachi], by the Kamakura Citizen's Net, accessed on September 30, 2008 * {{cite book | last = Kusumoto | first = Katsuji | coauthors = | title = Kamakura Naruhodo Jiten | publisher = Jitsugyō no Nihonsha | date = July 2002 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | id = ISBN 978-4-408-00779-3 }} * Papinot, E. On June 30, 1203 ([[Shōji]] 1, 12th day of the 4th month) his remaining powers were formally taken from him and assumed by a council of 13 elders headed by his grandfather [[Hōjō Tokimasa]]. Yoriie in turn plotted with the Hiki to subjugate the Hōjō clan but failed, was put under house arrest, forced to abdicate and eventually assassinated on July 17, 1204 in Izu. Yoriie was succeeded by his younger brother Sanetomo, the last of the [[Seiwa Genji]] line to rule, at least nominally, over Kamakura. == Hiki Yoshikazu's rebellion== {{main|Hiki Yoshikazu}} Seriously ill, Yoriie proposed to name both his younger brother [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]], and his young son (Hiki's grandson) [[Minamoto no Ichiman]] to succeed him; the two would split power, governing separate parts of the country. It seemed natural to them that Hiki would then be the regent, even if unofficially, of young Ichiman. Hiki suggested to Yoriie, who would be assassinated shortly afterwards by a separate faction (the [[Hōjō clan]], that they arrange to have Sanetomo killed. [[Hōjō Masako]], Yoriie's mother and wife of the first shogun Yoritomo, allegedly overheard the conversation. On a pretext, [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] invited Hiki Yoshikazu to his home and assassinated him. A battle between the clans ensued, the Hiki were defeated by a coalition of the Hōjō, [[Wada clan|Wada]], [[Miura clan|Miura]] and [[Hatakeyama clan]]s and exterminated.Kamakura Citizen's NetAccording to Japanese Wikipedia's "源一幡" (Minamoto no Ichiman) article, the [[Gukanshō]] and the [[Azuma Kagami]] give different versions of the events. This is the Azuma Kagami's version. According to the Gukanshō, when in 1203 Yoriie became seriously ill, the Hōjō clan supported his younger brother Senman (future third shogun Minamoto no Sanetomo) as a successor, while the Hiki supported son Ichiman. To avoid power falling into the hands of the Hiki, the Hōjō decided to get rid of them and their protege. This is the version of events described in the article [[Minamoto no Ichiman]].
According to the same Wikipedia article, the Azuma Kagami claims Ichiman was killed by the Hiki, and not by the Hōjō. Yoriie died in Shuzenji, a small town in today's [[Izu province]], assassinated by his uncle [[Hōjō Tokimasa]]. == Three sons of Yoriie== Yoriie had three sons, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], [[Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], and Senju-maru, all of whom died of a violent death, victims of the power struggle that followed Yoritomo's sudden death. Ichiman (1198–1203) was the eldest. His mother Wakasa no Tsubone was Hiki Yoshikazu's daughter, and the child was brought up by the Hiki clan. There are contrasting versions of his deatth, but in any case he died in the fire that destroyed the Hiki residence. Second son Yoshinari, the only one of the three to reach adulthood, was forced to become a bonze and in 1219 murdered his uncle Sanetomo on the stone stairs at Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū in the shogunal capital of Kamakura, an act for which he was himself slain on the same day.Kusumoto (2002: 70-73) Third son {{nihongo|Senju-maru|千寿丸|(1201-1214)}} was 12 when Izumi Chikahira rebelled against the Hōjō to make the child shogun. After Chikahira's defeat, the child was forced to become a bonze like his older brother Yoshinari. A year later [[Wada Yoshimori]] also rebelled but, like Chikahira, was defeated and Senju-maru died with the others of the Wada clan. ==Eras of Yoriie's ''bakufu''== The years in which Yoriie was shogun are all within only one [[Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[nengō]]'': ''[[Kennin]]'' (1201–1204). ==Notes== [[Image:Minamoto no Yoriies grave 2007-02-25.jpg|thumb|right|Minamoto no Yoriie's grave in Shuzenji, [[Izu, Shizuoka|Izu]]]] {{Reflist}} ==References== * {{cite book | last = Kamiya | first = Michinori | coauthors = | title = Fukaku Aruku - Kamakura Shiseki Sansaku Vol. 1 & 2 | publisher = Kamakura Shunshūsha | year = 2008 | location = Kamakura | language = Japanese | isbn = 4774003409 | oclc = 169992721 }} * {{cite book | last = Kusumoto | first = Katsuji | coauthors = | title = Kamakura Naruhodo Jiten | publisher = Jitsugyō no Nihonsha | date = July 2002 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | isbn = 978-4-408-00779-3 }} * Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [http://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&client=firefox-a ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[Harvard University Press]]. 10-ISBN 0-674-01753-6; 13-ISBN 978-0-674-01753-5; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128] * Papinot, Jacques Edmund Joseph. (1906) ''Dictionnaire d'histoire et de géographie du japon.'' Tokyo: Librarie Sansaisha.[http://www.unterstein.net/Toyoashihara-no-Chiaki-Nagaioaki-no-Mitsuho-no-Kuni/NobiliaireJapon.pdf ..Click link for digitized 1906 ''Nobiliaire du japon'' (2003)] * [[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] (1834). ''[[Nihon Odai Ichiran]]''; ou, [http://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&dq=nipon+o+dai+itsi+ran ''Annales des empereurs du Japon.''] Paris: Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland. [http://www.worldcat.org/title/nipon-o-dai-itsi-ran-ou-annales-des-empereurs-du-japon/oclc/5850691 OCLC 5850691]. * {{cite book | last = Yasuda | first = Motohisa (editor) | coauthors = | title = Kamakura, Muromachi Jinmei Jiten | publisher = Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha | year = 1990 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | isbn = 978-4-404-01757-4 | author = | oclc = 24654085 }} ==External links == * [http://www.kcn-net.org/e_kama_history/omachi/omachi_1.htm Ōmachi], by the Kamakura Citizen's Net, accessed on September 30, 2008 {{s-start}} {{succession box | title=[[Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogun]]:

{{Japanese name|Minamoto}} [[File:Minamoto no Yoriie.jpg|thumb|right||Minamoto no Yoriie]] {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yoriie'''|源 頼家||September 11, 1182 – August 14, 1204}} was the second [[shogun]] (1202–1203) of [[Japan]]'s [[Kamakura shogunate]], and the first son of first shogun [[Minamoto no Yoritomo|Yoritomo]].Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric. (2005). "Minamoto no Yoriie" in {{Google books|p2QnPijAEmEC|''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 635|page=635}}. == Life == Born from Tokimasa's daughter [[Hōjō Masako]] at [[Hiki Yoshikazu]]'s residence in [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura]], Yoriie had as wet nurses the wives of powerful men like Hiki himself and [[Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and Hiki's younger sister.Yasuda (1990:592-593) Before he was born, his father Yoritomo had [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] and his men carry stones to build the [[Tsurugaoka_Hachiman-gū#Wakamiya_.C5.8Cji|Dankazura]] on [[Wakamiya Ōji]] to pray for the child's safe delivery. When Yoriie later himself had an heir, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], the child was also born at the Hiki mansionThe mansion no longer exists and its location ({{coord|35|19|1.31|N|139|33|20.95|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:1500|display=inline}}) is occupied by Myōhon-ji, a temple dedicated to the memory of the Hiki clan, exterminated by the [[Hōjō clan]]. from Hiki's daughter Wakasa no Tsubone, a fact which further consolidating an already strong emotional bondKamiya Vol. 1 (2008:44-45) From this relationship Hiki gained considerable influence when Yoriie became shogun, raising the hostility of [[Hōjō Tokimasa]], who was instead close to Yoriie's younger brother Senman (future third shogun [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]]), and was in his turn trying to leverage that relationship for political advantage.Papinot (2972:385_386) After his father's death in 1199, the 17-year-old became head of the [[Minamoto]] clan and was appointed [[Shogun|''seii taishogun'']] in 1202. He was however criticized for his abandonment of his father's policies, and his mother forbade him to do any political activity. On June 30, 1203 ([[Shōji]] 1, 12th day of the 4th month) his remaining powers were formally taken from him and assumed by a council of 13 elders headed by his grandfather [[Hōjō Tokimasa]]. Yoriie in turn plotted with the Hiki to subjugate the Hōjō clan but failed, was put under house arrest, forced to abdicate and eventually assassinated on July 17, 1204 in Izu. Yoriie was succeeded by his younger brother Sanetomo, the last of the [[Seiwa Genji]] line to rule, at least nominally, over Kamakura. == Hiki Yoshikazu's rebellion== {{main|Hiki Yoshikazu}} Seriously ill, Yoriie proposed to name both his younger brother [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]], and his young son (Hiki's grandson) [[Minamoto no Ichiman]] to succeed him; the two would split power, governing separate parts of the country. It seemed natural to them that Hiki would then be the regent, even if unofficially, of young Ichiman. Hiki suggested to Yoriie, who would be assassinated shortly afterwards by a separate faction (the [[Hōjō clan]], that they arrange to have Sanetomo killed. [[Hōjō Masako]], Yoriie's mother and wife of the first shogun Yoritomo, allegedly overheard the conversation. On a pretext, [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] invited Hiki Yoshikazu to his home and assassinated him. A battle between the clans ensued, the Hiki were defeated by a coalition of the Hōjō, [[Wada clan|Wada]], [[Miura clan|Miura]] and [[Hatakeyama clan]]s and exterminated.Kamakura Citizen's NetAccording to Japanese Wikipedia's "源一幡" (Minamoto no Ichiman) article, the [[Gukanshō]] and the [[Azuma Kagami]] give different versions of the events. This is the Azuma Kagami's version. According to the Gukanshō, when in 1203 Yoriie became seriously ill, the Hōjō clan supported his younger brother Senman (future third shogun Minamoto no Sanetomo) as a successor, while the Hiki supported son Ichiman. To avoid power falling into the hands of the Hiki, the Hōjō decided to get rid of them and their protege. This is the version of events described in the article [[Minamoto no Ichiman]].
According to the same Wikipedia article, the Azuma Kagami claims Ichiman was killed by the Hiki, and not by the Hōjō. Yoriie died in Shuzenji, a small town in today's [[Izu province]], assassinated by his uncle [[Hōjō Tokimasa]]. == Three sons of Yoriie== Yoriie had three sons, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], [[Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], and Senju-maru, all of whom died of a violent death, victims of the power struggle that followed Yoritomo's sudden death. Ichiman (1198–1203) was the eldest. His mother Wakasa no Tsubone was Hiki Yoshikazu's daughter, and the child was brought up by the Hiki clan. There are contrasting versions of his deatth, but in any case he died in the fire that destroyed the Hiki residence. Second son Yoshinari, the only one of the three to reach adulthood, was forced to become a bonze and in 1219 murdered his uncle Sanetomo on the stone stairs at Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū in the shogunal capital of Kamakura, an act for which he was himself slain on the same day.Kusumoto (2002: 70-73) Third son {{nihongo|Senju-maru|千寿丸|(1201-1214)}} was 12 when Izumi Chikahira rebelled against the Hōjō to make the child shogun. After Chikahira's defeat, the child was forced to become a bonze like his older brother Yoshinari. A year later [[Wada Yoshimori]] also rebelled but, like Chikahira, was defeated and Senju-maru died with the others of the Wada clan. ==Eras of Yoriie's ''bakufu''== The years in which Yoriie was shogun are all within only one [[Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[nengō]]'': ''[[Kennin]]'' (1201–1204). ==Notes== [[Image:Minamoto no Yoriies grave 2007-02-25.jpg|thumb|right|Minamoto no Yoriie's grave in Shuzenji, [[Izu, Shizuoka|Izu]]]] {{Reflist}} ==References== * {{cite book | last = Kamiya | first = Michinori | coauthors = | title = Fukaku Aruku - Kamakura Shiseki Sansaku Vol. 1 & 2 | publisher = Kamakura Shunshūsha | year = 2008 | location = Kamakura | language = Japanese | isbn = 4774003409 | oclc = 169992721 }} * {{cite book | last = Kusumoto | first = Katsuji | coauthors = | title = Kamakura Naruhodo Jiten | publisher = Jitsugyō no Nihonsha | date = July 2002 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | isbn = 978-4-408-00779-3 }} * Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [http://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&client=firefox-a ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[Harvard University Press]]. 10-ISBN 0-674-01753-6; 13-ISBN 978-0-674-01753-5; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128] * Papinot, Jacques Edmund Joseph. (1906) ''Dictionnaire d'histoire et de géographie du japon.'' Tokyo: Librarie Sansaisha.[http://www.unterstein.net/Toyoashihara-no-Chiaki-Nagaioaki-no-Mitsuho-no-Kuni/NobiliaireJapon.pdf ..Click link for digitized 1906 ''Nobiliaire du japon'' (2003)] * [[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] (1834). ''[[Nihon Odai Ichiran]]''; ou, [http://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&dq=nipon+o+dai+itsi+ran ''Annales des empereurs du Japon.''] Paris: Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland. [http://www.worldcat.org/title/nipon-o-dai-itsi-ran-ou-annales-des-empereurs-du-japon/oclc/5850691 OCLC 5850691]. * {{cite book | last = Yasuda | first = Motohisa (editor) | coauthors = | title = Kamakura, Muromachi Jinmei Jiten | publisher = Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha | year = 1990 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | isbn = 978-4-404-01757-4 | author = | oclc = 24654085 }} ==External links == * [http://www.kcn-net.org/e_kama_history/omachi/omachi_1.htm Ōmachi], by the Kamakura Citizen's Net, accessed on September 30, 2008 {{s-start}} {{succession box | title=[[Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogun]]:

{{Japanese name|Minamoto}} [[File:Minamoto no Yoriie.jpg|thumb|right||Minamoto no Yoriie]] {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yoriie'''|源 頼家||September 11, 1182 – August 14, 1204}} was the second [[shogun]] (1202–1203) of [[Japan]]'s [[Kamakura shogunate]], and the first son of first shogun [[Minamoto no Yoritomo|Yoritomo]].Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric. (2005). "Minamoto no Yoriie" in {{Google books|p2QnPijAEmEC|''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 635|page=635}}. == Life == Born from Tokimasa's daughter [[Hōjō Masako]] at [[Hiki Yoshikazu]]'s residence in [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura]], Yoriie had as wet nurses the wives of powerful men like Hiki himself and [[Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and Hiki's younger sister.Yasuda (1990:592-593) Before he was born, his father Yoritomo had [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] and his men carry stones to build the [[Tsurugaoka_Hachiman-gū#Wakamiya_.C5.8Cji|Dankazura]] on [[Wakamiya Ōji]] to pray for the child's safe delivery. When Yoriie later himself had an heir, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], the child was also born at the Hiki mansionThe mansion no longer exists and its location ({{coord|35|19|1.31|N|139|33|20.95|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:1500|display=inline}}) is occupied by Myōhon-ji, a temple dedicated to the memory of the Hiki clan, exterminated by the [[Hōjō clan]]. from Hiki's daughter Wakasa no Tsubone, a fact which further consolidating an already strong emotional bondKamiya Vol. 1 (2008:44-45) From this relationship Hiki gained considerable influence when Yoriie became shogun, raising the hostility of [[Hōjō Tokimasa]], who was instead close to Yoriie's younger brother Senman (future third shogun [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]]), and was in his turn trying to leverage that relationship for political advantage.Papinot (2972:385_386) After his father's death in 1199, the 17-year-old became head of the [[Minamoto]] clan and was appointed [[Shogun|''seii taishogun'']] in 1202. He was however criticized for his abandonment of his father's policies, and his mother forbade him to do any political activity. On June 30, 1203 ([[Shōji]] 1, 12th day of the 4th month) his remaining powers were formally taken from him and assumed by a council of 13 elders headed by his grandfather [[Hōjō Tokimasa]]. Yoriie in turn plotted with the Hiki to subjugate the Hōjō clan but failed, was put under house arrest, forced to abdicate and eventually assassinated on July 17, 1204 in Izu. Yoriie was succeeded by his younger brother Sanetomo, the last of the [[Seiwa Genji]] line to rule, at least nominally, over Kamakura. ==Hiki Yoshikazu's rebellion== {{main|Hiki Yoshikazu}} Seriously ill, Yoriie proposed to name both his younger brother [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]], and his young son (Hiki's grandson) [[Minamoto no Ichiman]] to succeed him; the two would split power, governing separate parts of the country. It seemed natural to them that Hiki would then be the regent, even if unofficially, of young Ichiman. Hiki suggested to Yoriie, who would be assassinated shortly afterwards by a separate faction (the [[Hōjō clan]], that they arrange to have Sanetomo killed. [[Hōjō Masako]], Yoriie's mother and wife of the first shogun Yoritomo, allegedly overheard the conversation. On a pretext, [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] invited Hiki Yoshikazu to his home and assassinated him. A battle between the clans ensued, the Hiki were defeated by a coalition of the Hōjō, [[Wada clan|Wada]], [[Miura clan|Miura]] and [[Hatakeyama clan]]s and exterminated.Kamakura Citizen's NetAccording to Japanese Wikipedia's "源一幡" (Minamoto no Ichiman) article, the [[Gukanshō]] and the [[Azuma Kagami]] give different versions of the events. This is the Azuma Kagami's version. According to the Gukanshō, when in 1203 Yoriie became seriously ill, the Hōjō clan supported his younger brother Senman (future third shogun Minamoto no Sanetomo) as a successor, while the Hiki supported son Ichiman. To avoid power falling into the hands of the Hiki, the Hōjō decided to get rid of them and their protege. This is the version of events described in the article [[Minamoto no Ichiman]].
According to the same Wikipedia article, the Azuma Kagami claims Ichiman was killed by the Hiki, and not by the Hōjō. Yoriie died in Shuzenji, a small town in today's [[Izu province]], assassinated by his uncle [[Hōjō Tokimasa]]. ==Three sons of Yoriie== Yoriie had three sons, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], [[Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], and Senju-maru, all of whom died of a violent death, victims of the power struggle that followed Yoritomo's sudden death. Ichiman (1198–1203) was the eldest. His mother Wakasa no Tsubone was Hiki Yoshikazu's daughter, and the child was brought up by the Hiki clan. There are contrasting versions of his death, but in any case he died in the fire that destroyed the Hiki residence. Second son Yoshinari, the only one of the three to reach adulthood, was forced to become a bonze and in 1219 murdered his uncle Sanetomo on the stone stairs at Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū in the shogunal capital of Kamakura, an act for which he was himself slain on the same day.Kusumoto (2002: 70-73) Third son {{nihongo|Senju-maru|千寿丸|(1201-1214)}} was 12 when Izumi Chikahira rebelled against the Hōjō to make the child shogun. After Chikahira's defeat, the child was forced to become a bonze like his older brother Yoshinari. A year later [[Wada Yoshimori]] also rebelled but, like Chikahira, was defeated and Senju-maru died with the others of the Wada clan. ==Eras of Yoriie's ''bakufu''== The years in which Yoriie was shogun are all within only one [[Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[nengō]]'': ''[[Kennin]]'' (1201–1204). ==Notes== [[Image:Minamoto no Yoriies grave 2007-02-25.jpg|thumb|right|Minamoto no Yoriie's grave in Shuzenji, [[Izu, Shizuoka|Izu]]]] {{Reflist}} ==References== * {{cite book | last = Kamiya | first = Michinori | coauthors = | title = Fukaku Aruku - Kamakura Shiseki Sansaku Vol. 1 & 2 | publisher = Kamakura Shunshūsha | year = 2008 | location = Kamakura | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-7740-0340-9 | oclc = 169992721 }} * {{cite book | last = Kusumoto | first = Katsuji | coauthors = | title = Kamakura Naruhodo Jiten | publisher = Jitsugyō no Nihonsha | date = July 2002 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | isbn = 978-4-408-00779-3 }} * Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [http://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&client=firefox-a ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[Harvard University Press]]. 10-ISBN 0-674-01753-6; 13-ISBN 978-0-674-01753-5; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128] * Papinot, Jacques Edmund Joseph. (1906) ''Dictionnaire d'histoire et de géographie du japon.'' Tokyo: Librarie Sansaisha.[http://www.unterstein.net/Toyoashihara-no-Chiaki-Nagaioaki-no-Mitsuho-no-Kuni/NobiliaireJapon.pdf ..Click link for digitized 1906 ''Nobiliaire du japon'' (2003)] * [[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] (1834). ''[[Nihon Odai Ichiran]]''; ou, [http://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&dq=nipon+o+dai+itsi+ran ''Annales des empereurs du Japon.''] Paris: Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland. [http://www.worldcat.org/title/nipon-o-dai-itsi-ran-ou-annales-des-empereurs-du-japon/oclc/5850691 OCLC 5850691]. * {{cite book | last = Yasuda | first = Motohisa (editor) | coauthors = | title = Kamakura, Muromachi Jinmei Jiten | publisher = Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha | year = 1990 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | isbn = 978-4-404-01757-4 | author = | oclc = 24654085 }} ==External links== * [http://www.kcn-net.org/e_kama_history/omachi/omachi_1.htm Ōmachi], by the Kamakura Citizen's Net, accessed on September 30, 2008 {{s-start}} {{succession box | title=[[Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogun]]:
Minamoto no Yoriie | before=[[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] | after=[[Minamoto no Sanetomo]] | years=1202–1203}} {{s-end}} {{Persondata | NAME = Minamoto no Yoriie | ALTERNATIVE NAMES = | SHORT DESCRIPTION = 2nd [[shogun]] of [[Japan]]'s [[Kamakura shogunate]] | DATE OF BIRTH = September 11, 1182 | PLACE OF BIRTH = | DATE OF DEATH = August 14, 1204 | PLACE OF DEATH = }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Minamoto no, Yoriie}} [[Category:1182 births]] [[Category:1204 deaths]] [[Category:Kamakura shoguns]] [[Category:Minamoto clan]] [[Category:People of Heian period Japan]] [[Category:People of Kamakura period Japan]] [[ca:Minamoto no Yoriie]] [[de:Minamoto no Yoriie]] [[es:Minamoto no Yoriie]] [[eu:Minamoto no Yoriie]] [[fr:Minamoto no Yoriie]] [[ko:미나모토노 요리이에]] [[it:Minamoto no Yoriie]] [[nl:Minamoto no Yoriie]] [[ja:源頼家]] [[pl:Yori'ie Minamoto]] [[ru:Минамото-но Ёрииэ]] [[sv:Minamoto no Yoriie]] [[uk:Мінамото но Йоріїе]] [[zh:源賴家]]

[http://www.worldcat.org/title/nipon-o-dai-itsi-ran-ou-annales-des-empereurs-du-japon/oclc/5850691 OCLC 5850691]. * {{cite book | last = Yasuda | first = Motohisa (editor) | coauthors = | title = Kamakura, Muromachi Jinmei Jiten | publisher = Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha | year = 1990 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | isbn = 978-4-404-01757-4 | author = | oclc = 24654085 }} ==External links== * [http://www.kcn-net.org/e_kama_history/omachi/omachi_1.htm Ōmachi], by the Kamakura Citizen's Net, accessed on September 30, 2008 {{s-start}} {{succession box | title=[[Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogun]]:

revision.text:
{{Japanese name|Minamoto}} [[File:Minamoto no Yoriie.jpg|thumb|right||Minamoto no Yoriie]] {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yoriie'''|源 頼家||September 11, 1182 – August 14, 1204}} was the second [[shogun]] (1202–1203) of [[Japan]]'s [[Kamakura shogunate]], and the first son of first shogun [[Minamoto no Yoritomo|Yoritomo]].Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric. (2005). "Minamoto no Yoriie" in {{Google books|p2QnPijAEmEC|''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 635|page=635}}. == Life == Born from Tokimasa's daughter [[Hōjō Masako]] at [[Hiki Yoshikazu]]'s residence in [[Kamakura, Kanagawa|Kamakura]], Yoriie had as wet nurses the wives of powerful men like Hiki himself and [[Kajiwara Kagetoki]], and Hiki's younger sister.Yasuda (1990:592-593) Before he was born, his father Yoritomo had [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] and his men carry stones to build the [[Tsurugaoka_Hachiman-gū#Wakamiya_.C5.8Cji|Dankazura]] on [[Wakamiya Ōji]] to pray for the child's safe delivery. When Yoriie later himself had an heir, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], the child was also born at the Hiki mansionThe mansion no longer exists and its location ({{coord|35|19|1.31|N|139|33|20.95|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:1500|display=inline}}) is occupied by Myōhon-ji, a temple dedicated to the memory of the Hiki clan, exterminated by the [[Hōjō clan]]. from Hiki's daughter Wakasa no Tsubone, a fact which further consolidating an already strong emotional bondKamiya Vol. 1 (2008:44-45) From this relationship Hiki gained considerable influence when Yoriie became shogun, raising the hostility of [[Hōjō Tokimasa]], who was instead close to Yoriie's younger brother Senman (future third shogun [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]]), and was in his turn trying to leverage that relationship for political advantage.Papinot (2972:385_386) After his father's death in 1199, the 17-year-old became head of the [[Minamoto]] clan and was appointed [[Shogun|''seii taishogun'']] in 1202. He was however criticized for his abandonment of his father's policies, and his mother forbade him to do any political activity. On June 30, 1203 ([[Shōji]] 1, 12th day of the 4th month) his remaining powers were formally taken from him and assumed by a council of 13 elders headed by his grandfather [[Hōjō Tokimasa]]. Yoriie in turn plotted with the Hiki to subjugate the Hōjō clan but failed, was put under house arrest, forced to abdicate and eventually assassinated on July 17, 1204 in Izu. Yoriie was succeeded by his younger brother Sanetomo, the last of the [[Seiwa Genji]] line to rule, at least nominally, over Kamakura. ==Hiki Yoshikazu's rebellion== {{main|Hiki Yoshikazu}} Seriously ill, Yoriie proposed to name both his younger brother [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]], and his young son (Hiki's grandson) [[Minamoto no Ichiman]] to succeed him; the two would split power, governing separate parts of the country. It seemed natural to them that Hiki would then be the regent, even if unofficially, of young Ichiman. Hiki suggested to Yoriie, who would be assassinated shortly afterwards by a separate faction (the [[Hōjō clan]], that they arrange to have Sanetomo killed. [[Hōjō Masako]], Yoriie's mother and wife of the first shogun Yoritomo, allegedly overheard the conversation. On a pretext, [[Hōjō Tokimasa]] invited Hiki Yoshikazu to his home and assassinated him. A battle between the clans ensued, the Hiki were defeated by a coalition of the Hōjō, [[Wada clan|Wada]], [[Miura clan|Miura]] and [[Hatakeyama clan]]s and exterminated.Kamakura Citizen's NetAccording to Japanese Wikipedia's "源一幡" (Minamoto no Ichiman) article, the [[Gukanshō]] and the [[Azuma Kagami]] give different versions of the events. This is the Azuma Kagami's version. According to the Gukanshō, when in 1203 Yoriie became seriously ill, the Hōjō clan supported his younger brother Senman (future third shogun Minamoto no Sanetomo) as a successor, while the Hiki supported son Ichiman. To avoid power falling into the hands of the Hiki, the Hōjō decided to get rid of them and their protege. This is the version of events described in the article [[Minamoto no Ichiman]].
According to the same Wikipedia article, the Azuma Kagami claims Ichiman was killed by the Hiki, and not by the Hōjō. Yoriie died in Shuzenji, a small town in today's [[Izu province]], assassinated by his uncle [[Hōjō Tokimasa]]. ==Three sons of Yoriie== Yoriie had three sons, [[Minamoto no Ichiman|Ichiman]], [[Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]], and Senju-maru, all of whom died of a violent death, victims of the power struggle that followed Yoritomo's sudden death. Ichiman (1198–1203) was the eldest. His mother Wakasa no Tsubone was Hiki Yoshikazu's daughter, and the child was brought up by the Hiki clan. There are contrasting versions of his death, but in any case he died in the fire that destroyed the Hiki residence. Second son Yoshinari, the only one of the three to reach adulthood, was forced to become a bonze and in 1219 murdered his uncle Sanetomo on the stone stairs at Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū in the shogunal capital of Kamakura, an act for which he was himself slain on the same day.Kusumoto (2002: 70-73) Third son {{nihongo|Senju-maru|千寿丸|(1201-1214)}} was 12 when Izumi Chikahira rebelled against the Hōjō to make the child shogun. After Chikahira's defeat, the child was forced to become a bonze like his older brother Yoshinari. A year later [[Wada Yoshimori]] also rebelled but, like Chikahira, was defeated and Senju-maru died with the others of the Wada clan. ==Eras of Yoriie's ''bakufu''== The years in which Yoriie was shogun are all within only one [[Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[nengō]]'': ''[[Kennin]]'' (1201–1204). ==Notes== [[Image:Minamoto no Yoriies grave 2007-02-25.jpg|thumb|right|Minamoto no Yoriie's grave in Shuzenji, [[Izu, Shizuoka|Izu]]]] {{Reflist}} ==References== * {{cite book | last = Kamiya | first = Michinori | coauthors = | title = Fukaku Aruku - Kamakura Shiseki Sansaku Vol. 1 & 2 | publisher = Kamakura Shunshūsha | year = 2008 | location = Kamakura | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-7740-0340-9 | oclc = 169992721 }} * {{cite book | last = Kusumoto | first = Katsuji | coauthors = | title = Kamakura Naruhodo Jiten | publisher = Jitsugyō no Nihonsha | date = July 2002 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | isbn = 978-4-408-00779-3 }} * Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric and Käthe Roth. (2005). [http://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC&client=firefox-a ''Japan encyclopedia.''] Cambridge: [[Harvard University Press]]. 10-ISBN 0-674-01753-6; 13-ISBN 978-0-674-01753-5; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128] * Papinot, Jacques Edmund Joseph. (1906) ''Dictionnaire d'histoire et de géographie du japon.'' Tokyo: Librarie Sansaisha.[http://www.unterstein.net/Toyoashihara-no-Chiaki-Nagaioaki-no-Mitsuho-no-Kuni/NobiliaireJapon.pdf ..Click link for digitized 1906 ''Nobiliaire du japon'' (2003)] * [[Isaac Titsingh|Titsingh, Isaac.]] (1834). ''[[Nihon Odai Ichiran]]''; ou, [http://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&dq=nipon+o+dai+itsi+ran ''Annales des empereurs du Japon.''] Paris: Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland. [http://www.worldcat.org/title/nipon-o-dai-itsi-ran-ou-annales-des-empereurs-du-japon/oclc/5850691 OCLC 5850691]. * {{cite book | last = Yasuda | first = Motohisa (editor) | coauthors = | title = Kamakura, Muromachi Jinmei Jiten | publisher = Shin Jinbutsu Ōraisha | year = 1990 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | isbn = 978-4-404-01757-4 | author = | oclc = 24654085 }} ==External links== * [http://www.kcn-net.org/e_kama_history/omachi/omachi_1.htm Ōmachi], by the Kamakura Citizen's Net, accessed on September 30, 2008 {{s-start}} {{succession box | title=[[Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogun]]:
Minamoto no Yoriie | before=[[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] | after=[[Minamoto no Sanetomo]] | years=1202–1203}} {{s-end}} {{Persondata | NAME = Minamoto no Yoriie | ALTERNATIVE NAMES = | SHORT DESCRIPTION = 2nd [[shogun]] of [[Japan]]'s [[Kamakura shogunate]] | DATE OF BIRTH = September 11, 1182 | PLACE OF BIRTH = | DATE OF DEATH = August 14, 1204 | PLACE OF DEATH = }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Minamoto no, Yoriie}} [[Category:1182 births]] [[Category:1204 deaths]] [[Category:Kamakura shoguns]] [[Category:Minamoto clan]] [[Category:People of Heian-period Japan]] [[Category:People of Kamakura-period Japan]] [[ca:Minamoto no Yoriie]] [[de:Minamoto no Yoriie]] [[es:Minamoto no Yoriie]] [[eu:Minamoto no Yoriie]] [[fr:Minamoto no Yoriie]] [[ko:미나모토노 요리이에]] [[it:Minamoto no Yoriie]] [[nl:Minamoto no Yoriie]] [[ja:源頼家]] [[pl:Yori'ie Minamoto]] [[ru:Минамото-но Ёрииэ]] [[sv:Minamoto no Yoriie]] [[th:มินะโมะโตะ โนะ โยะริอิเอะ]] [[uk:Мінамото но Йоріїе]] [[zh:源賴家]]

A year later [[Wada Yoshimori]] also rebelled but, like Chikahira, was defeated and Senju-maru died with the others of the Wada clan. ==Eras of Yoriie's ''bakufu''== The years in which Yoriie was shogun are all within only one [[Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[nengō]]'': ''[[Kennin]]'' (1201–1204). ==Notes== [[Image:Minamoto no Yoriies grave 2007-02-25.jpg|thumb|right|Minamoto no Yoriie's grave in Shuzenji, [[Izu, Shizuoka|Izu]]]] {{Reflist}} ==References== * {{cite book | last = Kamiya | first = Michinori | coauthors = | title = Fukaku Aruku - Kamakura Shiseki Sansaku Vol. 1 & 2 | publisher = Kamakura Shunshūsha | year = 2008 | location = Kamakura | language = Japanese | isbn = 4-7740-0340-9 | oclc = 169992721 }} * {{cite book | last = Kusumoto | first = Katsuji | coauthors = | title = Kamakura Naruhodo Jiten | publisher = Jitsugyō no Nihonsha | date = July 2002 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | isbn = 978-4-408-00779-3 }} * [[Louis-Frédéric|Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric]] and Käthe Roth.
