{{Infobox person|image=File:Minamoto no Yoriie.jpg|birth_date={{birth date|1182|9|11}}|death_date={{death date|1204|8|14}}|birth_place={{ill|Kamakura|qid=Q200267|1=Kamakura}}|death_place={{ill|Shuzen-ji|qid=Q11386345|1=Shuzen-ji}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17|1=Japan}}|occupation={{ill|bushi|qid=Q1975935|1=bushi}}; {{ill|samurai|qid=Q38142|1=Samurai}}|title={{ill|shogun|qid=Q131767|1=Shogun}}|spouse={{ill|Q106615814|qid=Q106615814|1=Q106615814}}; {{ill|Q106615820|qid=Q106615820|1=Q106615820}}; {{ill|Wakasa no tsubone|qid=Q498616|1=Wakasa no tsubone}}|children={{ill|Eijitsu|qid=Q11535661|1=Eijitsu}}; {{ill|Ichiman|qid=Q6863145|1=Minamoto no Ichiman}}; {{ill|Kugyō|1=Kugyō (priest)|2=ja|3=公卿|qid=Q6442006|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3138036}}; {{ill|Take no gosho|qid=Q497557|1=Take no gosho}}; {{ill|Zengyō|qid=Q11591185|1=Zengyō}}|father={{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}|mother={{ill|Hōjō Masako|qid=Q463961|1=Hōjō Masako}}|relatives={{ill|Jōgyō|qid=Q11634528|1=Jōgyō}}; {{ill|Minamoto no Sanetomo|qid=Q174249|1=Minamoto no Sanetomo}}; {{ill|Sanman|qid=Q11355364|1=Sanman}}; {{ill|Ōhime|qid=Q11434217|1=Ōhime}}|family={{ill|Kawachi Genji|qid=Q5360652|1=Kawachi Genji}}|native_name=源頼家|resting_place={{ill|Shuzen-ji|qid=Q11386345|1=Shuzen-ji}}}} {{Short description|Shogun of the Kamakura shogunate (r. 1202–03)}} {{family name hatnote|Minamoto|lang=Japanese}} {{Infobox officeholder | name = Minamoto no Yoriie
{{lang|ja|源頼家}} | image = Minamoto no Yoriie.jpg | caption = | office = ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|qid=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}}'' | monarch = {{ill|Emperor Tsuchimikado|en|Emperor Tsuchimikado|qid=Q357269|lt=Tsuchimikado|1=Emperor Tsuchimikado}} | predecessor = [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] | successor = [[Minamoto no Sanetomo]] | birth_date = {{birth date|1182|9|11}} | birth_place = {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|qid=Q200267|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | death_date = {{death date and age|1204|8|14|1182|9|11}} | death_place = | spouse = Wakasa no Tsubone | children = {{Plainlist| * {{ill|Minamoto no Ichiman|en|Minamoto no Ichiman|qid=Q6863145|lt=Minamoto no Ichiman|1=Minamoto no Ichiman}} * {{ill|Kugyō (priest)|en|Kugyō (priest)|qid=Q6442006|lt=Kugyō|1=Kugyō (priest)}} * Eijitsu * Zengyō * Take no gosho}} | term_start =1202 | term_end = 1203 | father = [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] | mother = {{ill|Hōjō Masako|en|Hōjō Masako|qid=Q463961|lt=Hōjō Masako|1=Hōjō Masako}} | signature = }} {{nihongo|'''Minamoto no Yoriie'''|源 頼家||September 11, 1182 – August 14, 1204|lead=yes}} was the second ''{{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|qid=Q131767|lt=Shogun|1=Shogun}}'' (1202–1203) of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}'s {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|qid=Q736839|lt=Kamakura shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}, and the first son of first shōgun [[Minamoto no Yoritomo|Yoritomo]].{{ill|Louis Frédéric|en|Louis Frédéric|qid=Q726040|lt=Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric|1=Louis Frédéric}}. (2005). "Minamoto no Yoriie" in {{Google books|p2QnPijAEmEC|''Japan Encyclopedia'', p. 635|page=635}}. His {{ill|Dharma name|en|Dharma name|qid=Q1543672|lt=Dharma name|1=Dharma name}} was Hokke-in-dono Kingo Da'i Zengo (法華院殿金吾大禅閤). == Life == Minamoto no Yoriie was born to {{ill|Hōjō Masako|en|Hōjō Masako|qid=Q463961|lt=Hōjō Masako|1=Hōjō Masako}} at {{ill|Hiki Yoshikazu|en|Hiki Yoshikazu|qid=Q5760562|lt=Hiki Yoshikazu|1=Hiki Yoshikazu}}'s residence in {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|qid=Q200267|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura}}.Yasuda (1990:592-593) Before he was born, his father Yoritomo had {{ill|Hōjō Tokimasa|en|Hōjō Tokimasa|qid=Q589811|lt=Hōjō Tokimasa|1=Hōjō Tokimasa}} and his men carry stones to build the [[Tsurugaoka Hachiman-gū#Wakamiya .C5.8Cji|Dankazura]] on {{ill|Wakamiya Ōji|en|Wakamiya Ōji|qid=Q7960797|lt=Wakamiya Ōji|1=Wakamiya Ōji}} to pray for the child's safe delivery. When Yoriie later himself had an heir, {{ill|Minamoto no Ichiman|en|Minamoto no Ichiman|qid=Q6863145|lt=Ichiman|1=Minamoto no Ichiman}}, the child was also born at the Hiki mansionThe mansion no longer exists and its location ({{coord|35|19|1.31|N|139|33|20.95|E|region:JP_type:landmark_scale:1500|display=inline}}) is occupied by {{ill|Myōhon-ji|en|Myōhon-ji|qid=Q11446912|lt=Myōhon-ji|1=Myōhon-ji}} a temple dedicated to the memory of the Hiki clan, exterminated by the {{ill|Hōjō clan|en|Hōjō clan|qid=Q834364|lt=Hōjō clan|1=Hōjō clan}}. to Hiki's daughter Wakasa no Tsubone, a fact which further consolidated an already strong emotional bond.Kamiya Vol. 1 (2008:44-45) From this relationship {{ill|Hiki clan|en|Hiki clan|qid=Q11547377|lt=Hiki|1=Hiki clan}} gained considerable influence when Yoriie became shōgun, incurring the hostility of {{ill|Hōjō Tokimasa|en|Hōjō Tokimasa|qid=Q589811|lt=Hōjō Tokimasa|1=Hōjō Tokimasa}}, who was instead close to Yoriie's younger brother Senman (future third shōgun [[Minamoto no Sanetomo|Sanetomo]]), and who was in his turn trying to leverage that relationship for political advantage. His childhood name was Manju (万寿). When he was young, he was interested in military arts like fencing, and horse-riding. After his father's death in 1199, the 17-year-old became head of the [[Minamoto clan|Minamoto]] clan and was appointed {{ill|Shogun|en|Shogun|qid=Q131767|lt=''sei-i taishōgun''|1=Shogun}} in 1202. He was, however, criticized for his abandonment of his father's policies, and his mother forbade him from any political activity. On June 30, 1203, his remaining powers were formally taken from him and assumed by a council of 13 elders, headed by his grandfather {{ill|Hōjō Tokimasa|en|Hōjō Tokimasa|qid=Q589811|lt=Hōjō Tokimasa|1=Hōjō Tokimasa}}. He ordained as a Buddhist monk. Yoriie, in turn, plotted with the Hiki to subjugate the {{ill|Hōjō clan|en|Hōjō clan|qid=Q834364|lt=Hōjō|1=Hōjō clan}} clan. However, he failed, and was put under house arrest, where he was forced to abdicate. Then on July 17, 1204, he was assassinated in his mansion in Izu. Yoriie was succeeded by his younger brother Sanetomo, the last of the [[Minamoto clan|Minamoto]] shogun to rule over {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|qid=Q200267|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura}}. ==Hiki Yoshikazu's rebellion== {{main|Hiki Yoshikazu}} Seriously ill, Yoriie proposed to name both his younger brother, Minamoto no Sanetomo, and his young son {{ill|Minamoto no Ichiman|en|Minamoto no Ichiman|qid=Q6863145|lt=Minamoto no Ichiman|1=Minamoto no Ichiman}} to succeed him; the two would split power, governing separate parts of the country. It seemed natural to them that Hiki would then be the regent, even if unofficially, of young Ichiman. Hiki suggested to Yoriie that they arrange to have Sanetomo killed. {{ill|Hōjō Masako|en|Hōjō Masako|qid=Q463961|lt=Hōjō Masako|1=Hōjō Masako}}, Yoriie's mother and wife of the first shōgun Yoritomo, allegedly overheard the conversation. On a pretext, {{ill|Hōjō Tokimasa|en|Hōjō Tokimasa|qid=Q589811|lt=Hōjō Tokimasa|1=Hōjō Tokimasa}} invited {{ill|Hiki Yoshikazu|en|Hiki Yoshikazu|qid=Q5760562|lt=Hiki Yoshikazu|1=Hiki Yoshikazu}} to his home and assassinated him. A battle between the clans ensued, the Hiki were defeated by a coalition of the Hōjō, [[Wada clan|Wada]], {{ill|Miura clan|en|Miura clan|qid=Q2053762|lt=Miura|1=Miura clan}} and [[Hatakeyama]] clans and were exterminated. Yoriie died in Shuzenji, a small town in what was later called {{ill|Izu Province|en|Izu Province|qid=Q860928|lt=Izu Province|1=Izu Province}}, assassinated by his grandfather {{ill|Hōjō Tokimasa|en|Hōjō Tokimasa|qid=Q589811|lt=Hōjō Tokimasa|1=Hōjō Tokimasa}}. ==The sons of Yoriie== Yoriie had two sons, {{ill|Minamoto no Ichiman|en|Minamoto no Ichiman|qid=Q6863145|lt=Ichiman|1=Minamoto no Ichiman}} and [[:en:Kugyō (Minamoto no Yoshinari)|Kugyō]]. Both of his sons would end up dead, like their father they would fall victim to the power struggle that followed Yoritomo's death. Ichiman (1198–1203) was the eldest. His mother, Wakasa no Tsubone, was {{ill|Hiki Yoshikazu|en|Hiki Yoshikazu|qid=Q5760562|lt=Hiki Yoshikazu|1=Hiki Yoshikazu}}'s daughter, and the child was brought up by the {{ill|Hiki clan|en|Hiki clan|qid=Q11547377|lt=Hiki clan|1=Hiki clan}}. It is said that he died in a fire that destroyed the Hiki residence. Yoriie's second son Yoshinari, his only son to reach adulthood, was forced to become a Buddhist monk. In 1219, Yoshinari murdered his uncle Sanetomo on the stairs leading to the shrine of [[Tsurugaoka Hachimangū]] in the shogunal capital of {{ill|Kamakura|en|Kamakura|qid=Q200267|lt=Kamakura|1=Kamakura}}, an act for which he was himself slain on the same day.Kusumoto (2002: 70–73) ==Family== * Father: [[Minamoto no Yoritomo]] * Mother: {{ill|Hōjō Masako|en|Hōjō Masako|qid=Q463961|lt=Hōjō Masako|1=Hōjō Masako}} * Wife: Wakasa no Tsubone (d. 1203) * Children: ** {{ill|Minamoto no Ichiman|en|Minamoto no Ichiman|qid=Q6863145|lt=Minamoto no Ichiman|1=Minamoto no Ichiman}} ** {{ill|Kugyō (priest)|en|Kugyō (priest)|qid=Q6442006|lt=Kugyō|1=Kugyō (priest)}} ==Eras of Yoriie's ''bakufu''== The years in which Yoriie was shōgun are all within only one [[:en:Japanese era names|era name]] or ''[[:en:Nengō|Nengō]]'': ''{{ill|Kennin|en|Kennin|qid=Q1195433|lt=Kennin|1=Kennin}}'' (1201–1204). ==Notes== [[Image:Minamoto no Yoriies grave 2007-02-25.jpg|thumb|right|Minamoto no Yoriie's grave in Shuzenji, {{ill|Izu, Shizuoka|en|Izu, Shizuoka|qid=Q696840|lt=Izu|1=Izu, Shizuoka}}]] {{Reflist}} ==References== * {{cite book | last = Kamiya | first = Michinori | title = Fukaku Aruku: Kamakura Shiseki Sansaku Vol. 1 & 2 | publisher = Kamakura Shunshūsha | year = 2008 | location = Kamakura | language = Japanese | isbn = 978-4-7740-0340-5 | oclc = 169992721 }} * {{cite book | last = Kusumoto | first = Katsuji | title = Kamakura Naruhodo Jiten | publisher = Jitsugyō no Nihonsha | date = July 2002 | location = Tokyo | language = Japanese | isbn = 978-4-408-00779-3 }} * {{ill|Louis Frédéric|en|Louis Frédéric|qid=Q726040|lt=Nussbaum, Louis-Frédéric|1=Louis Frédéric}} and Käthe Roth. (2005). [https://books.google.com/books?id=p2QnPijAEmEC ''Japan encyclopedia''.] Cambridge: {{ill|Harvard University Press|en|Harvard University Press|qid=Q1587900|lt=Harvard University Press|1=Harvard University Press}}. {{ISBN|978-0-674-01753-5}}; [http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/58053128?referer=di&ht=edition OCLC 58053128] * {{ill|Edmond Papinot|en|Edmond Papinot|qid=Q4141631|lt=Papinot, Edmond|1=Edmond Papinot}}. (1906) ''Dictionnaire d'histoire et de géographie du japon''. Tokyo: Librarie Sansaisha.[http://www.unterstein.net/Toyoashihara-no-Chiaki-Nagaioaki-no-Mitsuho-no-Kuni/NobiliaireJapon.pdf ..Click link for digitized 1906 ''Nobiliaire du japon'' (2003)] * {{ill|Isaac Titsingh|en|Isaac Titsingh|qid=Q332779|lt=Titsingh, Isaac|1=Isaac Titsingh}}. (1834). ''[[Nihon Ōdai Ichiran]]''; ou, [https://books.google.com/books?id=18oNAAAAIAAJ&q=nipon+o+dai+itsi+ran ''Annales des empereurs du Japon''.] Paris: Royal Asiatic Society, Oriental Translation Fund of Great Britain and Ireland.
